data.title: Sverica Capital Management sells Med First, a healthcare company, after growing it from 13 to 27 clinics, quadrupling profitability.
data.description: Sverica Capital Management LP shared today the successful sale of RMS Healthcare Management, also known as "Med First." Under Sverica's ownership, Med First expanded its clinics from 13 to 27, with two more in the pipeline, and boosted profitability by almost fourfold. This growth elevated its status as a key player in North Carolina's primary care sector. Med First, operating since 2007, is based in Raleigh, NC.

data.title: Trially AI raises $4.7M in Seed Round, unveils "Margo" to address clinical trial recruitment obstacle.
data.description: In Kansas City, Missouri, Trially®, an AI-focused platform for clinical trial recruitment, has revealed that it successfully raised $4.7 million in funding. The company has also introduced Margo, an innovative AI system designed to enhance trial enrollment rates by transforming potential patient matches into active participants. Currently, clinical trial recruitment stands as a pressing issue in healthcare, with a significant 86% of trials experiencing delays due to recruitment issues. This setback has led to the pharmaceutical sector enduring daily losses exceeding $600,000. Through the utilization of Margo, Trially® aims to address these challenges and revolutionize the clinical trial recruitment process.

data.title: Families sue Boeing and Honeywell for catastrophic Air India crash, alleging a defective fuel cutoff switch led to 242 deaths.
data.description: Boeing Co. (NYSE: BA) is currently facing legal challenges as families of victims from a fatal crash in India have filed a lawsuit against the company along with Honeywell International Inc. (NASDAQ: HON). The lawsuit, filed in Delaware Superior Court, involves four victims from the Air India Flight 171 crash, which resulted in the deaths of 242 individuals onboard and 19 others on the ground. Allegations suggest that a faulty fuel cutoff switch led to the tragic incident in June. The lawsuit claims that based on flight data, a pilot unintentionally disabled fuel to the engines of the Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, resulting in a total loss of thrust shortly after takeoff. Furthermore, Boeing is set to deliver its first 737 Max aircraft to VietJet, nine years after the initial order was placed.

data.title: Schneider Electric unveils new AI infrastructure reference designs, supporting NVIDIA Mission Control and GB300 NVL72, targeting AI factories.
data.description: A groundbreaking AI infrastructure reference design incorporating controls has been introduced for optimal OT/IT interoperability with NVIDIA Mission Control and enterprise applications. This innovation, in collaboration with NVIDIA, features the cutting-edge NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 power and cooling reference design, essential for the rollout of future Blackwell GPUs. These dual reference designs empower data center operators to enhance the implementation speed of AI infrastructure for AI manufacturing facilities located anywhere.

data.title: Mill Creek starts preleasing at Modera Westside Trail in Atlanta, bringing 373 homes to West Midtown.
data.description: A new midrise community is set to add 373 homes to Atlanta's West Midtown neighborhood. The project, announced by Mill Creek Residential on September 18, 2025, marks a significant addition to the area. Mill Creek Residential is renowned for its focus on high-quality rental properties nationwide. This development is poised to provide a substantial housing boost to the Atlanta neighborhood, catering to the growing demand for modern living spaces.

data.title: XTM sells assets to Pateno Payments for $3 million, boosting fintech capabilities. #BusinessWire #Fintech
data.description: In Toronto, fintech company XTM Inc. (QB: XTMIF / CSE: PAID / FSE: 7XT) has completed the sale of its assets to Pateno Payments Inc., a Digital Commerce Group subsidiary, for a total of USD $3,000,000. The deal, originally announced on April 29, 2025, was finalized on September. XTM is known for its innovative services in automated tip pooling, instant payouts, and Earned Wage Access ("EWA") via its AnyDay™ platform.

data.title: Private 5G networks to surpass LTE with $5 billion market by 2028 as industry leaders drive Industry 4.0 adoption.
data.description: A database named "Private 5G Network Deployment Tracker & Forecasts 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings by SNS Telecom & IT. It predicts that annual investments in private 5G networks for vertical industries will see a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of around 41% from 2025 to 2028, reaching over $5 billion by the end of 2028. The "Private 5G Network Deployment Tracker & Forecasts: 2025-2030" datasheet contains a comprehensive database with more than 8,300 global entries.

data.title: ION Platform Finance US, Inc. and ION Platform Finance S.à r.l. have launched an exchange offer.
data.description: On September 18, 2025, ION Platform Finance US, Inc. and ION Platform Finance S.à r.l. announced a new joint venture. The companies are located at 1345 Avenue of the Americas, 49th Floor, New York, United States and 63-65 rue de Merl, L-2146, Luxembourg. The venture, in partnership with ION Trading Technologies S.à r.l., seeks to enhance financial services and technology solutions. This collaboration brings together resources from both countries to innovate and expand operations.

data.title: Banks predict gold rally to soar as Fed shifts focus. UBS raises forecast to $3,800/oz by 2025, Deutsche Bank to $4,000/oz by 2026. Fed cuts policy rate by 25 bps.
data.description: UBS and Deutsche Bank have both revised their forecasts for gold, citing reasons such as Federal Reserve easing, strong central bank demand, and a weakening U.S. dollar. UBS increased its gold price prediction by $300 to $3,800 per ounce by the end of 2025, and by $200 to $3,900 by mid-2026. Deutsche Bank, on the other hand, raised its 2026 average forecast to $4,000 per ounce, up from $3,700. The bank referred to fair value models indicating potential for further price increases due to excess demand from central banks and highlighted monetary policy independence risks. Furthermore, the Federal Open Market Committee recently reduced its policy rate by 25 basis points. This change marks the first rate cut of 2025 following a total reduction of 100 basis points.

data.title: Day Out Snacks introduces limited-edition fall and holiday flavors in response to consumer demand for variety beyond pumpkin spice.
data.description: Red Bank, New Jersey - September 18, 2025 - Day Out Snacks, a woman-owned snack brand, has released gluten-free, vegan, non-GMO protein balls to cater to consumer preferences for alternative seasonal flavors. Going beyond the popular pumpkin spice trend, the protein balls offer nostalgic taste profiles while maintaining a clean-label snacking experience. This move comes as a response to the increasing demand for diverse seasonal options.

data.title: Montreal-based digital agency Locomotive wins Awwwards Agency of the Year for 7th time and 2nd Webby Award.
data.description: In September 18, 2025, the Montreal-headquartered digital agency Locomotive achieved significant recognition. Earlier in February, the agency was named Agency of the Year on Awwwards for the seventh year running, marking an impressive streak. Furthermore, in the recent past, it secured a Webby Award for the second year in a row. This highlights Locomotive's consistent excellence in the digital sphere, showcasing their innovative and quality work.

data.title: Hong Kong's Cre8 Enterprise Limited (Nasdaq: CRE) is developing AI tools for translation, typesetting, proofreading, and design.
data.description: On September 18, 2025, Cre8 Enterprise Limited (Nasdaq: CRE), a financial printing service provider in Hong Kong, shared plans to create in-house artificial intelligence (AI) tools. These tools will aid in translation, typesetting, proofreading, and design within the company. By investing in AI technology internally, Cre8 aims to enhance its operational efficiency and service quality.

data.title: Dot Ai (Nasdaq: DAIC) secures $1.27M in new hardware platform orders, expanding Puerto Rico ops, showcasing growth.
data.description: Dot Ai, a leading IoT and AI-based SaaS company, has recently disclosed that it successfully secured $1.27 million in new orders for its hardware platform. This significant achievement highlights the company's strong growth momentum and serves as a testament to the rising market interest in Dot Ai's groundbreaking Asset Intelligence solutions. The influx of initial orders underscores the increasing demand for their services, prompting the need for expansion to cater to sustained customer interest.
